passage: For the linear function $g$, the graph of $y= g(x)$ in the $xy$-plane has a slope of 5 and passes through the point $(2,47)$. Which equation defines $g$?

passage:

passage: Exactly how many $x$-intercepts does the graph of $f(x) = x(x - 10)^2(x - 11)^3$ have in the $xy$-plane, where $y = f(x)$?

The given equation, where $t$ is a positive constant, defines a circle in the $xy$-plane. The radius of this circle is $\sqrt{41}$. What is the value of $t$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: In triangle RST, the measure of angle R is 29 degrees. Which statement provides sufficient information to prove that triangle RST is isosceles?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The measure of angle S is 151 degrees.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The measure of angle S is 61 degrees.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The measure of angle S is 58 degrees.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The measure of angle S is 29 degrees.

passage: If $a = 6k + 5r$ and $b = 9k - 12r + 3$, which expression is equivalent to $a - b$?

passage:

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: A polygonal chain is a connected series of line segments whose length is defined as the sum of the lengths of the line segments it is made of. A polygonal chain with a length of 33 centimeters (cm) is extended by adding line segments that each have a length of $8 - r$ cm. Which function $f$ represents the total length, in cm, of the extended polygonal chain after $x$ line segments are added?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: If $\frac{9}{6}(x-5)-36=\frac{7}{6}(x-5)+28$, what is the value of $x-5$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: $30$ grams of compound A, $45$ grams of compound B, and $x$ grams of compound C. The resulting mixture has a mass of $135$ grams. What is the value of $x$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: Line $k$ is shown in the $xy$-plane. Line $j$ (not shown) is perpendicular to line $k$ and passes through the point $(19, 23)$. What is the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept of line $j$?

skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: For a study, a group of chipmunks will be selected from a habitat consisting of 220 chipmunks, and a group of prairie dogs will be selected from a habitat consisting of 190 prairie dogs. Some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a treatment group, and some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a control group. Which of the following is necessary for this study to attempt to establish a cause-and-effect relationship between two variables?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The number of chipmunks in the treatment group is equal to the number of prairie dogs in the treatment group, and the number of chipmunks in the control group is equal to the number of prairie dogs in the control group.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The chipmunks and the prairie dogs are randomly assigned to the treatment and control groups.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The chipmunks and the prairie dogs are randomly selected from their respective habitats.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The average age of the chipmunks in the treatment group is equal to the average age of the prairie dogs in the treatment group, and the average age of the chipmunks in the control group is equal to the average age of the prairie dogs in the control group.

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: A certain investment account offers a special interest rate for the first 4 months the account is open followed by a lower interest rate for the remainder of the time the account is open. Akeyla opened one of these accounts with an original account balance of \$700.00 and did not make any other deposits or withdrawals. 4 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by 0.6% of the original balance. 6 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance at the end of the first 4 months. Every 2 months after the first 6 months, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance 2 months before. Which of the following equations could represent the account balance $B(x)$, in dollars, $x$ months after the account was opened, where $x \geq 4$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: As the size of a firework's shell increases, the height above the ground when the firework bursts increases. For a firework to burst 1,075 feet above the ground, a firework's shell in the shape of a sphere with a volume of 542.68 cubic inches could be used. To the nearest whole number, what is the volume of this firework's shell in $\text{cubic centimeters}$? (1 inch = 2.54 centimeters)

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: A right rectangular prism has a base area of $16t$ square centimeters (cm²). The length of the base of the rectangular prism is $\frac{8}{3}$ cm, and the height of the rectangular prism is 9 cm. Which expression represents the surface area, in cm², of the right rectangular prism?
